content: Just as the linguistic concept of reflexion (reflection) as a core vocabulary term for human learning in high English curricula frames structured metacognition as a teachable, graded skill, the Reflexion autonomous agent framework can borrow this human educational structure to improve LLM self-reflection mechanisms by scaffolding incremental reflection practice aligned with human cognitive learning patterns. This means LLM self-reflection does not need to be a one-step emergent check, but can be mapped to the sequential stages of human reflective writing practice that language learners are taught to build metacognitive accuracy.
